method_atom: Numerical measurement of the fractal (scaling) dimensions of geometric interface curves — the boundary and bulk lines separating ordered domains — in a many-body system tuned to a scale-invariant critical point. The finding of several distinct fractal-dimension values for different classes of interface is interpreted by mapping them onto the spectrum of primary operators of the continuum conformally-invariant algebra that describes the critical point.
object_atom: The Z_4 spin-lattice Ashkin-Teller model at the Fateev-Zamolodchikov point on its self-dual critical line, described in the continuum limit by Z_4 parafermionic theory, and more broadly c=1 critical theories.
brief_reason: What carries across is the technique of measuring fractal dimensions of critical interfaces and classifying them against a critical algebra's operator content; what is domain-specific is the Ashkin-Teller/Z_4-parafermion/c=1 spin system.
